LAW AND MOTION 301, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO CHEVY CHASE BANK'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS IS GRANTED. THE LIS PENDENS IS EXPUNGED. PLAINTIFFS REAL PROPERTY CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY RES JUDICATA. IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING PROBABLE VALIDITY GIVEN THE LACK OF EVIDENCE REGARDING PROPER TENDER OR EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THE MERITS OF THE REAL PROPERTY CLAIMS. DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO RECOVER THEIR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES IN AN AMOUNT TO BE DETERMINED BY A SEPARATE MOTION, IF THE PARTIES CANNOT REACH AN AGREEMENT AS TO THE AMOUNT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189

THE AMENDED COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. EVEN IF THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S PRIOR DECLARATION, IT DOES NOT ESTABLISH THAT THE CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST TWO CAUSES OF ACTION. RES JUDICATA BARS THIS ACTION. (SEE MALKOSKIE V. OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORP., (2010) 188 CAL.APP.4TH 968). THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IN ORDER FOR THE PLAINTIFFS TO ADDRESS THE INCONSISTENCES, IF ANY, BETWEEN THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBIT B TO THE COMPLAINT AND FOR PLAINTIFFS TO ALLEGE A DEFINITE AND SUBSTANTIAL CHANGE IN THEIR POSITION AS A RESULT OF THE ALLEGED PROMISE BY DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Jirn, Esq., from Archer Norris, 925-930-6600, for Defendant and moving party
Howard Olsen, Esq., Law Office, 415-317-7169, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO CHEVY CHASE BANK, F.S.B.'s DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The demurrer is overruled. The amended complaint states a cause of action for promissory estoppel. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of plaintiff's prior declaration, it does not establish that the cause of action fails. Defendant to file an answer on or before January 30, 2012. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Oct-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. HOWARD OLSEN, TEL. NO. 317-7169 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMES J. JIRN OF ARCHER NORRIS, TEL. NO. (925) 952-5511 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO CHEVY CHASE BANK'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES is Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $7,000.00 which is determined to be a reasonable fee for the lis pendens motion and this motion based on defense counsel's hourly rates (i.e. roughly 20 hours of compensable work). Much of the work on the lis pendens motion was taken from work done in the prior case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. HOWARD OLSEN, TEL. NO. 317-7169 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOUGLAS C. STRAUS OF ARCHER NORRIS, TEL. NO. (925) 930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Capital One's Demurrer to Complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first two causes of action. Res judicata bars this action. (See Malkoskie v. Option One Mortgage Corp., (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 968). The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with 10 days leave to amend in order for the Plaintiffs to address the inconsistences, if any, between the complaint and Exhibit B to the complaint and for Plaintiffs to allege a definite and substantial change in their position as a result of the alleged promise by Defendant. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

Notice And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. HOWARD OLSEN, TEL. NO. 418-8679 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOUGLAS C. STRAUS OF ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (925) 930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO CHEVY CHASE BANK'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens IS GRANTED. THE LIS PENDENS IS EXPUNGED. PLAINTIFFS REAL PROPERTY CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY RES JUDICATA. IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING PROBABLE VALIDITY GIVEN THE LACK OF EVIDENCE REGARDING PROPER TENDER OR EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THE MERITS OF THE REAL PROPERTY CLAIMS. DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO RECOVER THEIR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES IN AN AMOUNT TO BE DETERMINED BY A SEPARATE MOTION, IF THE PARTIES CANNOT REACH AN AGREEMENT AS TO THE AMOUNT. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
